description_long: This bungalow features a broad, low-pitched hip roof with deep overhangs and a prominent central chimney. The exterior walls are clad in a dark material, likely wood or brick, with lighter trim around the windows and roof edges. A wide, covered front porch with steps and railings extends across the main facade, providing a welcoming entryway. Multiple windows are arranged symmetrically on the front and sides, with some grouped together and others set individually. The roof includes several intersecting hips and a secondary, smaller roof section at the rear, adding architectural interest. The overall design is compact and horizontal, typical of early 20th-century Craftsman bungalows.
description: A dark, hip-roofed bungalow with a wide front porch, central chimney, and symmetrical window arrangement. The structure is compact, with intersecting rooflines and detailed trim.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide covered porch, central steps, and symmetrical window placement.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the deep roof overhangs, intersecting hip roofs, and side porch structure.
description_view_2: Rear view with a smaller roof section, back porch, and fewer windows compared to the front.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the extended porch structure and the complex roof geometry.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with grouped side windows
